content: 6 min read Just now -- Press enter or click to view image in full size You have used every one of these eleven pieces this week. Possibly today. Possibly in the last hour. And there is a real chance you could not fully explain at least three of them if someone asked you to, right now, without opening a tab to check. That is not an insult. It is just what happens when a framework works well enough that you stop needing to understand it to use it. I want to change that for you today, one honest explanation at a time, for the eleven pieces every real Angular app is actually built from. A quick note before we start, “components” in this title means the building blocks of Angular itself, not only the @Component decorator. Component, directive, pipe, service, and the rest, are the actual components of the framework you use every single day. 1. The Component, Beyond the Decorator Most developers describe a component as “a class with an @Component decorator." That is true, but incomplete. A component is really a contract between three things working together, a template, a class, and metadata connecting them. @Component({ selector: 'user-badge', template: `{{ name() }}` }) export class UserBadgeComponent { name =…
